title: QUIC RFC 9002 Appendix A Helper Recovery-State Verification
verification_method: Requirement-home execution, deterministic fuzz/property tests, existing BenchmarkDotNet helper-suite evidence, and SpecTrace/render inspection for the bounded RFC 9002 Appendix A helper subset.
preconditions: The selected Appendix A clauses remain present in `SPEC-QUIC-RFC9002`.
The current helper surfaces remain available: `QuicRttEstimator`, `QuicRecoveryTiming`, `QuicAckGenerationState`, and `QuicCongestionControlState`.
The existing RTT and congestion BenchmarkDotNet suites can run without benchmark source edits.
scope: This artifact verifies only the helper-backed Appendix A subset: SAP2, SAP7, SAP8 except SAP8-0005, and SAP10. It does not verify the deferred sender-ledger, timer-owner, PTO-probe, anti-amplification-unblock, or key-discard Appendix A clauses.
expected_result: The targeted requirement-home tests pass for all 23 selected clauses, deterministic fuzz top-offs exercise representative RTT adjustment, timer selection, and loss-delay helper arithmetic, existing RTT and congestion BenchmarkDotNet Dry runs validate the permanent helper performance suites, SpecTrace render/check stays current, and the known-baseline core SpecTrace profile remains classified as standing canonical backlog rather than a protocol failure.
procedure: Run the repo-local SpecTrace render/check flow for the changed canonical JSON artifacts.

Run `pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/Validate-SpecTraceJson.ps1 -Profiles core` and preserve the output even though the profile currently fails on the standing canonical backlog.
status_summary: Passed on 2026-04-23 for the bounded helper-backed Appendix A subset. The focused requirement-home run passed 73/73 tests, deterministic fuzz top-offs covered the shared RTT adjustment, loss-detection timer priority, and loss-delay helper arithmetic seams, and BenchmarkDotNet Dry passed for the existing RTT and congestion helper suites. SpecTrace render/check and diff-check passed. The core SpecTrace profile still failed with the known standing canonical backlog, now reported as 6218 errors after adding this traced artifact set; that output is preserved as evidence and is not classified as a protocol failure.
